---
title: "NTK-Mirror"
subtitle: "LoRA-free forward-pass fine-tuning for frozen causal language models"
---

`ntkmirror` adapts frozen Hugging Face causal LMs **without any weight
updates**. It learns a sparse *signed log-gate controller* — a few thousand
scalar gates that multiplicatively rescale selected decoder-layer channels:

$$
h'_{\ell,t,c} = e^{\,s_{\ell,c}}\, h_{\ell,t,c},
\qquad |s_{\ell,c}| \le s_{\max}.
$$

The controller is a small, detachable artifact: it can be trained in
minutes, saved to a file, composed with other controllers in log-gate
space, stored in a retrievable persistent memory, and attached per-request
at serving time — all while the base model stays untouched.

## Start here

- **[Technical report](ntkmirror_technical_report.qmd)** — a full
  reverse-engineering of the scientific method behind the repository: the
  controller mechanism, the activation-control NTK dual diagnostics,
  composition, persistent memory, the ISR hallucination verifier, the
  serving runtime, and the benchmark protocol, organized as a ledger of
  falsifiable hypotheses and the instruments that can refute them.

## Method notes

The original design notes that ship with the package:

| Note | Topic |
|---|---|
| [Method note](method.md) | The deployable controller and its failure modes |
| [Activation-control NTK duality](activation_control_ntk.md) | When gate updates can realize full-weight SGD fields |
| [Controller composability](composability.md) | Adding controllers in signed log-gate space |
| [Persistent memory controllers](persistent_memory.md) | Controllers as retrievable behavioral memories |
| [Persistent memory benchmark](public_memory_benchmark.md) | The five-condition benchmark protocol and its controls |
| [ISR verifier & KV order-debias](isr_kv_verifier.md) | Order-sensitivity as a hallucination signal |
| [V2 operating layer](v2_operating_layer.md) | Runtime isolation, artifact admission, memory governance |
| [Diffusion Adam-M scale gate](diffusion_scale_gate_adam_m.md) | A separate research harness and its failure analysis |

## Install and use

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dhavala-iitjmu/ntkmirror.git
cd ntkmirror
pip install -e .

ntkmirror fit --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --train train.jsonl --out controller.pt
ntkmirror eval --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --controller controller.pt --eval eval.jsonl
```

See the [README](https://github.com/dhavala-iitjmu/ntkmirror#readme) for
the full CLI reference and data formats.
